description: Lookups for nonexistent keys are never cached, so repeated absent-key requests penetrate to the datastore.
body: Failure mode: lookups for nonexistent keys are never cached, so repeated requests for absent data (often malicious) penetrate straight to the datastore on every call. Detection signals: high origin miss traffic for keys that never exist; datastore load from not-found queries; scanning patterns over random keys. Do not leave misses uncached on penetration-prone paths. Safer fallback: cache negative results with a short TTL or front the lookup with a Bloom filter, and invalidate the negative entry when the key is created.
